q: Which is a copyleft license (derivatives must stay under the same license)?
e: GNU GPL is the classic copyleft license. MIT, BSD, and Apache are permissive.

q: Which set are all permissive licenses?
e: MIT, BSD, and Apache are permissive. The GPL family is copyleft.

q: What does 'free as in freedom, not free as in beer' mean?
opts: It's about liberty/rights, not about price
Only free citizens may use it
e: It contrasts liberty (run, study, modify, share) with cost.

q: Which Linux application is used for raster image editing?
e: GIMP = raster/photo editing. Inkscape = vector, Blender = 3D, Audacity = audio.

q: Which application is the standard tool for vector graphics on Linux?
e: Inkscape handles vector graphics (SVG). GIMP is raster.

q: Which application is used for 3D modeling and animation?
e: Blender does 3D modeling, animation, and rendering.

q: Which two are common Linux web servers?
e: Apache and NGINX are the dominant Linux web servers.

q: What does ls -a show that plain ls hides?
opts: Hidden files (names starting with a dot)
e: -a = all, including hidden dotfiles like .bashrc.
-R = recursive: descends into every subdirectory.

opts: Long listing sorted by time, newest at the bottom
e: -l long, -t sort by time, -r reverse — newest files appear last.

q: In shell globbing, what does * match?
e: * matches zero or more characters.

q: In shell globbing, what does ? match?
e: ? matches exactly one character.
Brace expansion produces file1 file2 file3.

opts: Switches to your previous working directory
e: cd - returns to the previous directory.

q: What does -p do in mkdir -p a/b/c?
e: -p = parents: creates the full chain and won't error if it exists.

q: Which character continues a long command onto the next line?
e: A trailing backslash escapes the newline.

q: Which tar option compresses with gzip?
e: -z = gzip, -j = bzip2, -J = xz. -c only creates.

q: Does tar compress files by default?
opts: No — it only bundles unless you add a compression flag
e: tar does not compress by default. zip does.

q: In zip, which option gives the most compression?
e: -9 = maximum (slowest). -0 = none, default is -6.
-i = case-insensitive. -v inverts, -n line numbers, -R recurses.

q: Which file descriptor is STDERR, and how do you discard it?
e: STDIN=0, STDOUT=1, STDERR=2. 2>/dev/null discards errors.

opts: Sends STDOUT of first command into STDIN of second
e: A pipe connects commands: left STDOUT becomes right STDIN.

q: What three components make up any Linux distribution?
opts: The Linux kernel, GNU utilities, and a package manager
e: A distro = Linux kernel + GNU utilities + package manager.

q: Which distros belong to the Red Hat family?
e: Red Hat: Fedora (upstream) > RHEL > CentOS.
Tumbleweed and Kali are rolling releases.

q: How long is Ubuntu LTS supported?
e: Ubuntu LTS = 5 years support, released every 2 years.

opts: A virtual filesystem exposing kernel and process info
e: /proc is a pseudo-filesystem presenting live kernel data.

q: Where is DNS resolver configuration stored?
e: /etc/resolv.conf lists the nameservers.

q: What is the UID of the root user?
e: root is always UID 0. First regular user is UID 1000.
/etc/shadow holds password hashes (root-only).

q: Why can a normal user run passwd to change their password?
opts: The SUID bit makes it run with root's privileges
e: passwd has the SUID bit set, executing with owner's (root's) privileges.

q: What is chmod 640 in symbolic notation?
e: 640 = rw-r-----. 6=rw (owner), 4=r (group), 0=--- (others).

q: Which leading octal digit sets the sticky bit?
e: 1=sticky, 2=SGID, 4=SUID. Sticky on /tmp prevents deleting others' files.

q: How do adduser and useradd differ?
opts: adduser is interactive high-level; useradd is low-level
useradd is interactive; adduser is low-level
e: adduser is an interactive wrapper. useradd is the low-level command.

q: Which flag makes useradd create the home directory?
e: useradd -m creates the home directory. -d only sets its path.

opts: Locks the account (puts ! before the hash)
e: -l = lock: disables password login by prefixing hash with !.

q: What does umask 022 mean for new files?
e: umask subtracts from 666 for files: 666-022=644 (rw-r--r--).

q: What permission does the sticky bit on /tmp prevent?
opts: Users deleting files they don't own
e: Sticky bit means only file owner (or root) can delete their files in that directory.

q: What does chown user:group file do?
opts: Changes owner and group of the file
e: chown changes ownership. user:group sets both. -R for recursive.
